title: Consonants: Throat Sounds (कवर्ग)
description: Welcome to Hindi consonants (व्यंजन)! Hindi organizes its consonants into logical groups based on WHERE in your mouth they are produced. The first group is 'कवर्ग' — the VELAR consonants, made at the back of your throat (the soft palate). These are: क (ka), ख (kha), ग (ga), घ (gha), and ङ (nga). You already learned about aspirated pairs — now see them in their natural family!
keyPoints: Hindi consonants are organized into 5 groups (वर्ग) by mouth position
कवर्ग = velar group: sounds made at the back of the throat
5 letters: क (ka), ख (kha), ग (ga), घ (gha), ङ (nga)
Pattern: unaspirated → aspirated → unaspirated voiced → aspirated voiced → nasal
ङ (nga) is rare as a standalone letter but common in combinations

title: The 5-Letter Pattern of Every Consonant Group
explanation: Each consonant group in Hindi follows the same pattern of 5 letters: (1) unaspirated unvoiced, (2) aspirated unvoiced, (3) unaspirated voiced, (4) aspirated voiced, (5) nasal. For the throat group: क (ka, no voice, no air) → ख (kha, no voice, with air) → ग (ga, voiced, no air) → घ (gha, voiced, with air) → ङ (nga, nasal). This pattern repeats for ALL 5 consonant groups!
simpleRule: Every group has 5 letters in order: plain → aspirated → voiced → voiced+aspirated → nasal. कवर्ग: क ख ग घ ङ

vietnamese: ka — không bật hơi, vô thanh (như 'k' trong 'skip')

english: kha — aspirated, unvoiced (like 'k' in 'kite' + air burst)
vietnamese: kha — bật hơi, vô thanh (như 'k' trong 'kite' + luồng hơi)
ga — không bật hơi, hữu thanh (như 'g' trong 'go')

english: gha — aspirated, voiced ('g' in 'go' + air burst)
vietnamese: gha — bật hơi, hữu thanh ('g' trong 'go' + luồng hơi)
nga — âm mũi ngạc mềm (như 'ng' trong 'sing')

contextSentenceTranslation: The lotus blooms in the pond.
memoryTrick: KAMAL — the lotus is India's national flower! Think 'calm-al' sitting peacefully on water.
KHAR-gosh — feel the air burst on 'KH'! Think 'a rabbit that goes KHARGE(d) forward'.

hint: Start with the vertical line, add the curved hook on the right, and draw the headline (शिरोरेखा) on top.
Like a vertical line with two small curves. Draw the headline on top, then the body with its characteristic shape.

memoryTrick: GAAY — the cow is sacred in India! Think 'GAY' (happy) cow giving milk.
GHAR — feel the aspirated 'GH' with the air burst! Think 'a GAR(age) is part of a house'.

hint: A simple shape: vertical line with a horizontal stroke going left. Add the headline on top.
Like the number ४ with an extra loop. Draw the main body and add the headline on top.

prompt: What is the correct order of the कवर्ग consonants?
text: कवर्ग व्यंजनों का सही क्रम क्या है?
subtext: Remember the pattern: plain → aspirated → voiced → voiced+aspirated → nasal

title: Throat Sounds (कवर्ग) — Key Takeaways
takeaways: कवर्ग is the first consonant group — velar (throat) sounds
5 letters in order: क (ka), ख (kha), ग (ga), घ (gha), ङ (nga)
Pattern: unaspirated → aspirated → voiced → voiced+aspirated → nasal
This same 5-letter pattern repeats in ALL consonant groups
Key words: कमल (lotus), खरगोश (rabbit), गाय (cow), घर (house)
ङ (nga) is rare alone but sounds like 'ng' in 'sing'
All these sounds are made at the back of the throat (soft palate)
